what: Compute Building Metrics for an AOI — building_metrics
title: Compute Building Metrics for an AOI — building_metrics
text: function wrapper around osm_metrics() processes output building_structure(). calculates geometry-based building metrics per category provides structured summary downstream analysis within URBANmetRics workflow.

title: Compute Building Metrics for an AOI — building_metrics
Compute Building Metrics for an AOI — building_metrics
text: building_result sf object returned building_structure(), containing building features category column. aoi sf polygon representing Area Interest. Must valid sf object.

title: Compute Building Metrics for an AOI — building_metrics
text: named list containing: AOI AOI area m² km² summary Total building area share AOI categories data frame building metrics per category returned osm_metrics()

title: Compute Building Metrics for an AOI — building_metrics
text: function: projects AOI building features metric CRS (EPSG:3857), computes category-level metrics using osm_metrics(), calculates total building area share AOI, returns structured list containing AOI information, summary statistics, category-level metrics. function provides building-specific metrics layer URBANmetRics workflow. classify buildings (handled building_structure()), instead computes geometric density-based metrics building category. modular design ensures: consistent metric definitions across domains, reproducibility, separation concerns structure extraction metric computation.

title: Compute Building Metrics for an AOI — building_metrics
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ buildings <- building_structure(aoi) metrics <- building_metrics(buildings, aoi) metrics$summary } # }

what: Extract Building Features for an AOI Using Predefined OSM Tags — building_structure
title: Extract Building Features for an AOI Using Predefined OSM Tags — building_structure
text: function wrapper around osm_structure() provides predefined set OSM building tags (building_tags). represents building-specific entry point URBANmetRics workflow. function retrieves OSM building features within Area Interest (AOI), harmonizes core extraction engine, applies building classification routine.

title: Extract Building Features for an AOI Using Predefined OSM Tags — building_structure
Extract Building Features for an AOI Using Predefined OSM Tags — building_structure
text: aoi sf polygon representing Area Interest. Must valid sf object.

title: Extract Building Features for an AOI Using Predefined OSM Tags — building_structure
text: sf object containing building features intersecting AOI, including: geometry Spatial geometry building feature building_class Assigned building class classify_buildings() category Alias building_class, used consistency area Feature area (polygonal) length Feature length (linear) count Feature count (always 1 per row) original OSM attributes OSM key–value pairs returned Overpass

title: Extract Building Features for an AOI Using Predefined OSM Tags — building_structure
text: function: calls osm_structure() predefined building_tags, filters validates returned OSM features, applies classify_buildings() assign building classes, returns clean sf object building categories metrics. function intended used standalone OSM extractor. Instead, provides standardized building-specific wrapper around osm_structure(). modular design ensures: consistent preprocessing across thematic domains, reproducible building classification, separation concerns extraction interpretation.

text: named list three character vectors.

title: Predefined OSM green-space tags — green_tags
text: list contains three OSM keys commonly associated vegetation, parks, natural areas, forms urban green space: leisure OSM leisure=* values representing recreational green areas parks, gardens, allotments, nature reserves. landuse OSM landuse=* values representing vegetated land uses forest, meadow, orchard, cemetery. natural OSM natural=* values representing natural vegetation types wood, grassland, scrub, heath.

what: Compute Basic OSM Metrics for an AOI — osm_metrics
title: Compute Basic OSM Metrics for an AOI — osm_metrics
text: function calculates core geometric density-based metrics OSM features extracted osm_structure(). represents metrics-level foundation URBANmetRics workflow. Wrapper functions building_metrics(), green_metrics(), transport_metrics() build function compute domain-specific indicators.

title: Compute Basic OSM Metrics for an AOI — osm_metrics
Compute Basic OSM Metrics for an AOI — osm_metrics
text: osm_raw sf object returned osm_structure(), containing OSM features category column. aoi sf polygon representing Area Interest. Must valid sf object.

title: Compute Basic OSM Metrics for an AOI — osm_metrics
text: data frame containing aggregated OSM metrics per category, including: category Feature category assigned structure wrapper Area_m2 Total area polygonal features Length_m Total length linear features Count Total feature count Area_Share_percent Share total area across categories Length_Share_percent Share total length across categories Count_Share_percent Share total count across categories Area_Share_AOI_percent Area share relative AOI size Length_Share_AOI_percent Length share relative AOI size Count_Share_AOI_percent Count share relative total count Density_m2_per_km2 Area density per km² Density_count_per_km2 Count density per km²

title: Compute Basic OSM Metrics for an AOI — osm_metrics
text: function: projects AOI OSM features metric CRS (EPSG:3857), computes feature-level area, length, count, aggregates metrics category, calculates category shares (relative AOI-based), computes density metrics per km² function core metrics engine URBANmetRics workflow. apply thematic interpretation. Instead, provides standardized, geometry-based metrics table used domain-specific metrics wrappers. modular design ensures: consistent metric definitions across domains, reproducibility, separation concerns extraction, structure, metrics.

title: Compute Basic OSM Metrics for an AOI — osm_metrics
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ raw <- osm_structure(aoi, tags = building_tags) metrics <- osm_metrics(raw, aoi) } # }

what: Query and Prepare OSM Features for a Given AOI — osm_structure
title: Query and Prepare OSM Features for a Given AOI — osm_structure
text: function queries OpenStreetMap (OSM) features given Area Interest (AOI) using set user-defined OSM tag specifications. serves core extraction engine URBANmetRics workflow. Wrapper functions building_structure(), green_structure(), transport_structure() rely function retrieve preprocess OSM data specific thematic domains.

title: Query and Prepare OSM Features for a Given AOI — osm_structure
Query and Prepare OSM Features for a Given AOI — osm_structure
text: aoi sf polygon representing Area Interest. Must valid sf object. tags named list defining OSM key–value pairs extract. list element corresponds OSM key. Values may : TRUE match values key, character vector specific OSM tag values.

title: Query and Prepare OSM Features for a Given AOI — osm_structure
text: sf object containing OSM features match tag specification intersect AOI. returned object includes: geometry Spatial geometry (POINT, LINESTRING, POLYGON, etc.) category Assigned category based tag list area Feature area (polygons) length Feature length (lines) count Feature count (always 1 per row) original OSM attributes (e.g., building, landuse, highway, etc.)

title: Query and Prepare OSM Features for a Given AOI — osm_structure
text: function: queries multiple Overpass API fallback servers, retrieves matching geometries (points, lines, polygons, multipolygons), harmonizes attribute columns across geometry types, repairs invalid geometries, casts geometry types necessary (e.g., MULTILINESTRING → LINESTRING), computes basic metrics (area, length, count), assigns feature categories based provided tag list, filters results AOI. function intentionally designed generic OSM extraction framework. classify features . Instead, wrapper functions provide thematic tag sets post-processing logic. modular design ensures: reproducibility, extensibility, consistent preprocessing across thematic domains.

title: Query and Prepare OSM Features for a Given AOI — osm_structure
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Example: extract all buildings in an AOI tags <- list(building = TRUE) buildings <- osm_structure(aoi, tags) } # }

what: Compute Transport Metrics for an AOI — transport_metrics
title: Compute Transport Metrics for an AOI — transport_metrics
text: function wrapper around osm_metrics() processes output transport_structure(). calculates geometry-based transport metrics per category provides structured summary downstream analysis within URBANmetRics workflow.

title: Compute Transport Metrics for an AOI — transport_metrics
Compute Transport Metrics for an AOI — transport_metrics
text: transport_result sf object returned transport_structure(), containing transport features category column. aoi sf polygon representing Area Interest. Must valid sf object.

title: Compute Transport Metrics for an AOI — transport_metrics
text: named list containing: AOI AOI area m² km² summary Total transport area, length, count, AOI shares categories data frame transport metrics per category returned osm_metrics()

title: Compute Transport Metrics for an AOI — transport_metrics
text: function: projects AOI transport features metric CRS (EPSG:3857), computes category-level metrics using osm_metrics(), calculates total transport area, length, feature count, returns structured list containing AOI information, summary statistics, category-level metrics. function provides transport-specific metrics layer URBANmetRics workflow. classify transport features (handled transport_structure()), instead computes geometric density-based metrics transport category. Transport metrics differ building green metrics linear features (e.g., roads, railways, cycleways) play dominant role. Therefore, summary includes total length length-based density indicators. modular design ensures: consistent metric definitions across domains, reproducibility, separation concerns structure extraction metric computation.

title: Compute Transport Metrics for an AOI — transport_metrics
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ transport <- transport_structure(aoi) metrics <- transport_metrics(transport, aoi) metrics$summary } # }

title: Extract Transport Features for an AOI Using Predefined OSM Tags — transport_structure

Extract Transport Features for an AOI Using Predefined OSM Tags — transport_structure

title: Extract Transport Features for an AOI Using Predefined OSM Tags — transport_structure

Instead, provides standardized transport-specific wrapper around osm_structure(). modular design ensures: consistent preprocessing across thematic domains, reproducible transport classification, separation concerns extraction interpretation

title: Predefined OSM transport tags — transport_tags
Predefined OSM transport tags — transport_tags
text: named list three logical TRUE entries.

title: Predefined OSM transport tags — transport_tags
text: list contains three OSM keys commonly associated transport infrastructure, including roads, railways, public transport elements: highway Logical TRUE, meaning highway=* values matched. railway Logical TRUE, meaning railway=* values matched. public_transport Logical TRUE, meaning public_transport=* values matched.

title: Predefined OSM transport tags — transport_tags

what: Compute Urban Indices for Building, Green, and Transport Metrics — urban_index
title: Compute Urban Indices for Building, Green, and Transport Metrics — urban_index
text: function calculates comprehensive set urban indices based building, green, transport metrics. represents final step URBANmetRics workflow expects preprocessed metric tables input.

title: Compute Urban Indices for Building, Green, and Transport Metrics — urban_index
Compute Urban Indices for Building, Green, and Transport Metrics — urban_index
text: building_metrics data frame containing building-related metrics, including area shares, floor area density (m²/km²), building counts. green_metrics data frame containing green area metrics, including area shares category-level green information. transport_metrics data frame containing transport metrics, including lengths, counts, transport categories.

title: Compute Urban Indices for Building, Green, and Transport Metrics — urban_index
text: named list containing: BuildingIndex Weighted building index (0.7 area, 0.3 mass) BuildingIndex_area Area-based building intensity BuildingIndex_m2 Mass-based building intensity BuildingIndex_structure Morphological building structure index BuildingDiversityIndex Shannon diversity building categories GreenIndex Combined green index GreenIndex_area Area-based green intensity GreenIndex_balance Green–building balance index TransportIndex Transport index (length + count) SustainableTransportScore Share sustainable transport modes UrbanBalanced Balanced urban index UrbanLivability Livability index UrbanAccessibility Accessibility index

title: Compute Urban Indices for Building, Green, and Transport Metrics — urban_index
text: function returns: Building Index (area- mass-based) Green Index (area-based balance-based) Transport Index (length count) Sustainable Transport Score Building Diversity Index (Shannon diversity) Combined Urban Indices (Balanced, Livability, Accessibility)

title: Compute Urban Indices for Building, Green, and Transport Metrics — urban_index
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ui <- urban_index(building_metrics, green_metrics, transport_metrics) ui$UrbanBalanced ui$BuildingIndex ui$GreenIndex } # }
